question_text: The gravitational field strength on Earth's surface is approximately 10 N/kg. The gravitational field strength decreases with distance from Earth. At what distance would you expect the gravitational field strength to be approximately 2.5 N/kg?

options.label: A
options.text: At twice the distance from Earth's centre

options.label: B
options.text: At four times the distance from Earth's centre

options.label: C
options.text: At half the distance from Earth's centre

options.label: D
options.text: At one quarter of the distance from Earth's centre

explanation: Gravitational field strength follows the inverse square law — when you double the distance, the field strength becomes one quarter as strong. Since 10 N/kg ÷ 4 = 2.5 N/kg, the distance must be doubled (or approximately 2 Earth radii from the surface).
